title: VerilogAMS Support Features of Verilator
body:
Verilator released the new version 5.021 last January 17, 2024. It supports some Verilog-AMS function, for example "wreal". I wanted to investigate the specification if we can we use it for analog model simulation. Can you provide me working example of Verilog-AMS that can run in Verilator including testbench? I also need to know the language limitations. I already read the released document but with minimal discussions about Verilog-AMS.  Thank you.

Verilator has implemented a very small subset of Verilog AMS (Analog and Mixed-Signal Extensions) with the subset corresponding to those VMS keywords with near-equivalents in Verilog 2005 or SystemVerilog 2017 [1]. 

     AMS parsing is enabled with --language VAMS or --language 1800+VAMS.
     Verilator implements ceil, exp, floor, ln, log, pow, sqrt, string, and wreal.

The Verilator documentation also provides information on simulating Verilog models using Verilated-Model Runtime [2]. However, we could not find any information on whether Verilator supports the “wreal” function for analog model simulation.

[1] “Input Languages — Verilator Devel 5.021 documentation,” verilator.org. https://verilator.org/guide/latest/languages.html (accessed Jan. 19, 2024).
[2] “Simulating (Verilated-Model Runtime) — Verilator Devel 5.021 documentation,” verilator.org. https://verilator.org/guide/latest/simulating.html (accessed Jan. 19, 2024).
